metadata.special_note: WEEKEND EVAL: April 25 2026 is a Saturday. Indian markets closed. PM prediction file for 2026-04-24 does not exist — no PM prediction was generated on Friday April 24. AM prediction (2026-04-25_am.json) was generated Saturday 8:30 AM IST and predicts for Monday April 28, 2026 — actual outcome is NOT yet known at time of this evaluation. PM scoring is N/A (missing file). AM scoring is PENDING (outcome unavailable). This eval documents the pre-market quality assessment of the AM prediction only.

pm_scoring.reason: File 2026-04-24_pm.json does not exist. No PM prediction was generated on Friday April 24, 2026. PM scoring cannot be performed without the source prediction file.
pm_scoring.action_required: Investigate why PM agent did not run on 2026-04-24. Check scheduler logs. If PM agent skips Fridays by design when next day is a weekend, this is expected behavior — document it. If it was an execution failure, fix the scheduler.

am_scoring.pre_quality_note: PRE-OUTCOME QUALITY ASSESSMENT (not a score): The AM prediction demonstrates strong internal consistency. The PARTIAL_REVERSAL call is well-reasoned: (1) Gap-up source correctly identified as US-tech specific (Intel earnings) rather than geopolitical relief — prevents overfitting to Pattern 2 (ceasefire rally). (2) FOMC Day 1 caution as reversal trigger is well-timed for Monday afternoon. (3) FII reversal signal correctly flipped R4 auto-trigger — dynamic adjustment shows model responsiveness. (4) Max pain gravity at 24,000 coheres with WAIT_15_MIN approach. Main risk to prediction: if FII continues buying on Monday (second consecutive day), the PARTIAL_REVERSAL may not materialize and market may sustain above 24,200.

factor_analysis.note: Factor analysis is based on the AM prediction (2026-04-25_am.json) which predicts for April 28. Outcome-based classification (PERFECT/OVERWEIGHTED/etc.) cannot be determined until April 28 close. The following is a PRE-OUTCOME structural assessment of factor weights for model calibration review.

factors.pre_outcome_assessment: Weight 10 appropriate for AM mode where Gift Nifty is the definitive gap anchor. Intel +23% driving Nasdaq +1.63% to record is a clear, high-conviction bullish signal. No calibration concern flagged.

Weight reduced from 10 to 8 correctly as overnight window passed without escalation. PRE still active for intraday is accurate — Iran PRE has a 35% intraday escalation scenario. Weight 8 during a Trump-active intraday session is defensible. Could be argued as 7 if no new Iran headlines emerge by Monday open.

Brent at $105.33 (+0.30% — stable, not spiking). Weight 9 was justified by the Iran war active boost. However, crude is not actively escalating — it is holding, not rising. A weight of 7-8 might better reflect a stable-but-elevated crude vs a rising crude. Watch if Monday crude moves: if stable, weight 9 will look slightly overweighted.
factors.calibration_flag: MILD — crude stable vs escalating; confirm weight 9 applies only when crude is rising, not merely elevated

factors.pre_outcome_assessment: Critical dynamic adjustment: R4 auto-trigger correctly reset after FII returned to net buying (+₹999cr on April 24). Signal flip from BEARISH to MILDLY_BULLISH is well-supported — first net buying in 7 weeks is a structural change. If FII continues buying on Monday, weight 7 at MILDLY_BULLISH will be underweighted and should be 8-9 BULLISH.
Nikkei +0.97%, KOSPI +0.90%, Hang Seng -0.16%. Weight 7 appropriate for AM mode with known Asian prints. Broad positive ex-Hong Kong is consistent with MILDLY_BULLISH. No calibration concern.

VIX at 19.71 — elevated but not panic zone. The AM VIX reading is slightly higher than Friday's 18.36 (prediction says 19.71 but pm_data.json shows 18.36 on Friday). The 19.71 reading appears to be the Saturday morning Gift Nifty-implied VIX estimate. NEUTRAL_BEARISH appropriate for 18-25 range. Weight 7 defensible.
factors.data_flag: Verify if VIX 19.71 is Saturday Gift Nifty-implied or Friday close — pm_data.json shows 18.36 at Friday close

factors.pre_outcome_assessment: FOMC April 28-29. R1 partial: VIX fails condition 1 (not below 14), consensus hold meets condition 2. Weight 5 is consistent with PM assessment. BEARISH_CAUTIONARY for afternoon FOMC caution is well-calibrated — aligns with WAIT_15_MIN suggestion and expected PM fade.

USD/INR at 94.26 — historically weak. War-amplified bearish signal is appropriate. FII net buying despite this level suggests USD returns concern is not deterring all FII. Weight 6 is reasonable but could see downgrade to 5 if FII continues buying regardless of INR.

Iran war active since Feb 28. Regime multiplier driving weight from base 3 to 7 is mechanically correct. The regime correctly colors all other factors. Weight 7 appropriate as long as war regime is active.

Next weekly expiry May 2 (4 days away). Max pain at 24,000 creating gravity is a sound structural observation. Weight 5 for 3-7 days before expiry is per framework. The cohesion with WAIT_15_MIN (confirm hold above 24,000) is mechanically tight.

DII bought ₹13,490 crore on April 24. SIP mechanical floor is a structurally correct observation. Weight 4 (base weight, unchanged) is appropriate. Floor near 23,800-23,900 is coherent with 200-DMA at 23,530.

200-DMA at 23,530.43. After gap-up, Nifty would be +2.56% above 200-DMA — comfortably above. Reduced algorithmic sell-stop risk is accurate. Weight 3 appropriate as this is a background structural factor in this regime.
Correctly zeroed out at 8:30 AM IST as European markets not yet open. Framework correctly handled this timing limitation.

root_cause_for_missing_pm_prediction.technical_explanation: No PM prediction was generated on Friday April 24, 2026 (2026-04-24_pm.json does not exist). This is the primary gap in today's evaluation. Two possible explanations: (1) PM agent correctly skipped generating a PM prediction on Friday when the next trading day is Monday April 28 — the AM agent instead generated a weekend AM prediction for Monday. (2) PM agent execution failed on April 24 and the run was lost. The existence of 2026-04-25_am.json (Saturday AM prediction for Monday) suggests option 1 may be by design.
root_cause_for_missing_pm_prediction.recommended_fix: Clarify agent scheduling: should PM agent run on Fridays predicting Monday open? Currently it appears PM agent was skipped on Friday April 24. Document the scheduling decision — if intentional, note that Friday PM → Monday open predictions are handled by Saturday AM agent instead. If unintentional, fix scheduler.

new_parameter_flags.description: Major US semiconductor/tech company earnings beats directly drive Indian IT sector gap-open direction, independent of broader macro or Iran regime headwinds
new_parameter_flags.applies_when: Major US semiconductor or large-cap tech company (Intel, Nvidia, AMD, TSMC, Microsoft, Google) reports earnings after US market close — result is available by India's AM prediction window at 8:30 AM IST
new_parameter_flags.transmission_mechanism: Strong semiconductor earnings → Nasdaq gap-up → Gift Nifty rises → Indian IT sector opens higher regardless of macro bearish signals. The transmission is direct via Gift Nifty channel. Intel +23% drove Nasdaq +1.63% and Gift Nifty +0.99% on April 25 morning.
new_parameter_flags.why_not_in_current_framework: Current framework has US_MARKETS_GIFT_NIFTY as a composite factor capturing both futures and sector earnings. A separate semiconductor earnings sub-factor would help distinguish: (a) broad S&P rally vs (b) narrow tech-sector rally. Narrow rallies have different sustainment profiles for Indian IT.

am_prediction_key_strengths: Correctly identified Intel +23% earnings as the gap-up driver — not confused with geopolitical relief, so correctly rejected Pattern 2 (ceasefire rally sustain)
FII R4 auto-trigger reset is dynamically applied — model correctly updated signal from BEARISH to MILDLY_BULLISH on first net buying day in 7 weeks
FOMC Day 1 caution as PM fade trigger is precisely timed — aligns with institutional de-risking behavior before Fed decisions
WAIT_15_MIN approach is internally coherent with max pain gravity at 24,000 and PARTIAL_REVERSAL bias
Explicitly called out that the gap-up is US-sector-specific (not broad macro risk-on), limiting rally sustainability — this is sophisticated pattern discrimination
am_prediction_key_risks: If FII continues net buying Monday (second consecutive day), PARTIAL_REVERSAL may not materialize — sustained FII buying would push toward SUSTAIN pattern
Crude at $105.33 was stable on Friday; if crude spikes Monday on Iran news, BEARISH factors amplify and market may not even sustain 24,000
India VIX reading of 19.71 in AM prediction vs 18.36 in pm_data.json Friday close needs verification — source of the 19.71 figure is unclear

summary_for_quarterly_review.prediction_quality: PM NOT_SCORED (file missing) | AM PENDING (April 28 outcome needed)
summary_for_quarterly_review.key_learning: April 24 (Friday) PM agent did not run — gap in scheduler coverage for week-end to week-start transitions. Saturday AM prediction for Monday is well-constructed with high internal consistency. FII R4 dynamic reset shows model adaptability.
factors_needing_attention: CRUDE_OIL: weight 9 applied when crude is stable-but-elevated (not rising) — may be overweighted in stable war-regime vs escalating crude
INDIA_VIX: verify data source for Saturday morning reading (19.71 vs Friday close 18.36)
